title: 22 Afghan-Pakistani Clerics Jointly Urge Ceasefire Extension; Truce Ends at Midnight
detail: On the final day of the five-day Eid ceasefire, a joint statement signed by 22 prominent religious clerics — 11 Pakistani and 11 Afghan — was released in Pashto urging both governments to extend the ceasefire and achieve a 'sustainable and dignified' end to the conflict. The clerics called for negotiations and warned that continued fighting would bring catastrophe to both Muslim nations. The statement represented a rare instance of cross-border religious consensus. Despite the appeal, Pakistan's government made no announcement of an extension. The ceasefire expired at midnight between March 23 and 24, with Pakistan's Foreign Ministry spokesperson Tahir Andrabi later confirming the pause 'has concluded midnight between, I think, 23rd and 24th March.' Saudi Arabia, Qatar, and Turkey — who brokered the original truce — were monitoring the situation but had not announced a new mediation framework.

title: UN Experts Warn of Imminent Ceasefire Collapse, Call for Permanent Peace Process
detail: UN human rights experts issued an urgent statement calling on both Pakistan and Afghanistan to 'commit to a permanent ceasefire, resolve root causes of conflict, and ensure accountability for violations of international law' as the Eid truce neared expiry. The statement, issued from Geneva, cited the March 16 Pakistani airstrike on the Omid Drug Rehabilitation Center (143+ killed) as evidence of severe civilian harm and potential war crimes. UN experts noted that since Pakistan launched Operation Ghazab-il-Haq on February 26, at least 289 civilians had been documented killed across Afghanistan, with approximately 115,000 people displaced. The UN called on all states with influence over the parties — including China, Saudi Arabia, Turkey, Qatar, and the United States — to use their leverage to prevent resumption of hostilities. Pakistani officials responded that operations were targeted at terrorist infrastructure and would continue if TTP attacks persisted.
